page: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores via hyperthreading on a Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The x86_64 architecture includes 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor features a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

This server includes 2835 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and supports Premium Storage. In memory benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong performance, with small-block cached read bandwidth reaching 3733.43 GB/sec and large-block uncached read bandwidth at 259.05 GB/sec. Large-block uncached write bandwidth is measured at 106.22 GB/sec.

Compute benchmarks show strong single-core performance on stress-ng, though multi-core performance falls in the average tier. The server excels in database operations, with a strong PassMark database score and a Redis SET throughput of 84.18 million operations per second. Additionally, the hardware shows strong capabilities in low-parameter AI workloads, achieving top-tier text generation speeds of 332.7 tokens per second on a small 135M LLM model and strong performance on a medium 7B model.

From a resource density perspective, the D48ds_v7 offers a balanced allocation of compute, memory, and high-capacity local storage, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring local scratch space without the overhead of dedicated storage arrays. However, the average multi-core CPU benchmark relative to its 48 vCPUs suggests a potential tradeoff for highly parallelized, compute-bound tasks that do not leverage its specialized instruction sets or memory bandwidth. This instance is suited for relational databases, web serving, Redis caching layers, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 48 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database operations, including a Redis SET throughput of 84.18 million operations per second. It also achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference. While multi-core raw compute performance is average, the balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and high-capacity local storage provide a cost-efficient profile for database hosting, web serving, and caching workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for databases and caching.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring 48 Intel vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
2835 GB of local NVMe SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
Strong memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks
Top-tier text generation speeds for small-scale LLM inference
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and web serving
